content.problemStatement:
` It's your birthday today! So, you ask your dad to buy a brand new bicycle. But your dad knows you are weak in math. Hence he gives you the following task. If you solve it, he will purchase the bicycle you want! The task is:`

You are given a binary string. NNN denotes the length of a binary string and MMM denotes the number of occurrences of the character 111 in the string SSS.

Let F(S)F(S)F(S) be a function that gives the number of different substrings of string SSS, made up of the character 000 only.
Let Subl,rSub_{l,r}Subl,r​ denote the substring of SSS starting from lthl^{th}lth index and terminating at rthr^{th}rth index. Two substrings Subp,qSub_{p,q}Subp,q​ and Subr,sSub_{r,s}Subr,s​ are considered same if p=rp = rp=r and q=sq = sq=s otherwise the two substrings are considered different.

Among all possible strings SSS of length NNN having exactly MMM occurrences of the character 111, print the **minimum** possible value of F(S)F(S)F(S).


Input Format

First line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. Then the description of the TTT test case follows.
Each test case contains one line of input.
The first line contains two integers NNN and MMM.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line answer containing an integer.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, there exists only 3 binary strings of length NNN, having exactly MMM occurrences of the character 111.
For S=100S = 100S=100 value of F(S)=3F(S) = 3F(S)=3
For S=001S = 001S=001 value of F(S)=3F(S) = 3F(S)=3
For S=010S = 010S=010 value of F(S)=2F(S) = 2F(S)=2
So for string S=010S = 010S=010 we get a minimum value of F(S)=2F(S) = 2F(S)=2.
